More Staff
This clinic offers a welcoming space for transgender and non-binary people to receive culturally competent care from providers trained in transgender health needs. We provide several types of services:
Medication and hormone management
Mental health care
Referrals for procedures at contracted clinics
Case management services
Connection to community resources
Other gender-affirming care
Who:
Gender non-conforming, non-binary, and transgender clients ages 18 and older
When and
Where:
West County Health Center
, Every 1st Thursday morning
Martinez Health Center
, Every 2nd Thursday morning and 2nd Friday afternoon
Pittsburg Health Center
, Every 3rd Thursday morning
Appointments
The Gender Clinic is not a drop-in clinic, so you need an appointment to see us. You do not need a referral, but you may ask your primary care doctor to send one for you, and someone will contact you to set up the appointment. Alternatively, you may schedule an appointment for yourself by calling 1-800-495-8885.
For more information about the clinic, please contact Brooke Parker at
brooke.parker@cchealth.org
or call (925) 332-6087.
What to Expect
Before the Appointment
A week before your scheduled appointment, you will receive an appointment reminder by text or phone. Please confirm your appointment. If you are no longer able to attend, please cancel or reschedule your appointment.
A case manager will call you to ask about your preferred name and gender pronouns. They will also ask if there is anything specific you would like to talk about during your visit with the doctor.
During the Appointment
Contra Costa Health is a teaching system, and medical residents sometimes receive training at the Gender Clinic. Please let the clinic team know if you are not comfortable having a medical resident in the room during your visit. A case manager will also be present in the room to offer support for any non-medical needs.
New patients can expect to talk about...
Medical history and prior lab work
Family history
Social history
Supportive people in your life
Desires and expectations for care
Consent for treatment. This will include a conversation about how treatment may affect family planning
Options available for treatment
Returning patients can expect to talk about...
Any changes since your last visit, including visits to other doctors
Your current dose of hormones and your experience on the dose, if you are taking hormones
Laboratory (blood) test results
The status of scheduling gender-affirming surgery, if surgery is desired
After the Appointment
Follow-up (future) visits will be scheduled before you leave the office.
Please complete any required laboratory (blood) tests before your next appointment. You can find information on where to complete these tests
here
.
If your doctor would like you to see a specialist for additional care, a case manager will contact you to talk about this.
If your doctor has given you any new prescriptions, please have these filled at the pharmacy.

Letters of Support
The World Professional Association for Transgender Health (WPATH) released its Standards of Care version 8 in September 2022. WPATH no longer recommends requiring a support letter to approve or perform gender-affirming surgery, and neither the Contra Costa Regional Medical Center nor the Contra Costa Health Plan require a letter of support to provide gender-affirming surgery.
With that said, outside insurance companies and surgical providers may still require one or more letters of support to provide surgery, and different organizations may have different requirements for what must be included in a letter. Patients are encouraged to check with their surgical clinics for their requirements.
In most cases, one letter of support is needed for top surgery and two letters of support are needed for bottom surgery. These letters can be written by a medical provider or a mental health professional. Patients who are currently seeing a mental health professional and have a strong, trusted relationship with this professional are encouraged to ask them for a letter of support. If this is not the case for you, the Gender Clinic has a letter writer on staff who may be able to help.
For Providers
Contra Costa Health's
Gender Clinic staff are happy to assist you with general inquiries or needs you may have regarding a patient. Please message Brooke Parker or Adrienne Sofranko or use the resources below.
General Resources
All patients must provide informed consent for treatment prior to starting gender-affirming care.
Contra Costa Health informed consent forms are available on the CCH intranet (iSite).
